CRITICAL: The improved skill REGRESSED on aggregate pass rate (94.9% vs 97.4% for old skill). The delta is -2.5% — the improved skill is now WORSE by this measure. This is driven entirely by the SOFI options eval failure.

Eval 3 (SOFI) regression root cause: The improved skill's explicit instruction to avoid N/A for IV data backfired. The agent, following instructions more literally, returned N/A when it genuinely couldn't find IV Rank (behind paywalls) rather than estimating. The old skill's agent, without this instruction, freely estimated '~60-75%' from context. FIX: Change the instruction from 'don't return N/A' to 'if exact data unavailable, provide a best estimate with clear caveats (e.g., estimated ~60-70% based on recent 9.4% weekly drop and elevated VIX).'

Eval 3 Options Analysis section depth: The improved skill's options section was only ~18 lines vs threshold of 20. The improved skill consolidated options content into the Recommendation section and a Put-Selling Checklist bonus section instead. The old skill put everything in the Options Analysis section. FIX: Either lower the threshold back to 15, or instruct the skill to keep the full options analysis in the Options Analysis section rather than distributing it.

Eval 2 (GOLD) remains the improved skill's clearest win: historical valuation averages (13/13 vs 12/13). The improved skill's instruction to search for 'historical PE ratio 5 year average' is driving this. However, the old skill now passes the leverage quantification assertion that it failed in iteration-1, suggesting this was run variance, not a structural skill difference.

Eval 1 (MSFT) is completely non-discriminating at 16/16 for both. The iteration-1 IV data gap (old skill returned N/A, improved found data) did NOT reproduce — both configs found IV data this time. This suggests IV data availability is search-dependent, not skill-instruction-dependent.

27 of 42 assertions (64%) pass for BOTH configurations — still high non-discrimination, but improved from iteration-1's 94%. The 3 new discriminating assertions: (1) historical valuation averages in GOLD, (2) IV numeric data in SOFI, (3) options section >20 lines in SOFI. However, 2 of 3 discriminate in the WRONG direction (old skill wins).

The old skill's strength pattern is now clear: it produces longer, more expansive reports with creative bonus sections (Put-Selling Decision Framework, Stock Type Classification, 'Bottom Line for Your Question') and is more willing to estimate when data is uncertain. The improved skill is more structured and faster but sometimes too literal in following its own constraints.

RECOMMENDED SKILL CHANGES: (1) Replace 'Returning N/A is a failure mode' with 'If exact data unavailable, estimate with caveats', (2) Ensure Options Analysis section is self-contained (not distributed across other sections), (3) Consider incorporating the old skill's pattern of 'Bottom Line for Your Question' sections that directly address the user's specific ask
